title: Nuclear Monitoring Technicians
description: Collect and test samples to monitor results of nuclear experiments and contamination of humans, facilities, and environment.

tasks.text: Brief workers on radiation levels in work areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calculate safe radiation exposure times for personnel using plant contamination readings and prescribed safe levels of radiation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor personnel to determine the amounts and intensities of radiation exposure.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Inform supervisors when individual exposures or area radiation levels approach maximum permissible limits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide initial response to abnormal events or to alarms from radiation monitoring equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Determine intensities and types of radiation in work areas, equipment, or materials, using radiation detectors or other instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Instruct personnel in radiation safety procedures and demonstrate use of protective clothing and equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect samples of air, water, gases, or solids to determine radioactivity levels of contamination.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze samples, such as air or water samples, for contaminants or other elements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Determine or recommend radioactive decontamination procedures, according to the size and nature of equipment and the degree of contamination.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Set up equipment that automatically detects area radiation deviations and test detection equipment to ensure its accuracy.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare reports describing contamination tests, material or equipment decontaminated, or methods used in decontamination processes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place radioactive waste, such as sweepings or broken sample bottles, into containers for shipping or disposal.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Decontaminate objects by cleaning with soap or solvents or by abrading with wire brushes, buffing wheels, or sandblasting machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enter data into computers to record characteristics of nuclear events or to locate coordinates of particles.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Calibrate and maintain chemical instrumentation sensing elements and sampling system equipment, using calibration instruments and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
